proposed_statement_changes.current: For every sufficiently small delta != 0, P_{+,delta} is the nonlinear additive-Gaussian ANM X->Y and P_{-,delta} is the nonlinear additive-Gaussian ANM Y->X. Moreover V_y(P_{+,delta})=V_x(P_{-,delta})=0, V_x(P_{+,delta})=V_y(P_{-,delta})=C_m(delta)>0, so the stated score-variance functional identifies the experiment label h.
proposed_statement_changes.proposed: Suppose additionally that E[|g'(Z)|^4]<infinity. For every sufficiently small delta != 0, P_{+,delta} is the nonlinear additive-Gaussian ANM X->Y and P_{-,delta} is the nonlinear additive-Gaussian ANM Y->X. Moreover V_y(P_{+,delta})=V_x(P_{-,delta})=0, V_x(P_{+,delta})=V_y(P_{-,delta})=C_m(delta)>0, so the stated score-variance functional identifies the experiment label h.
proposed_statement_changes.reason: The exact forward non-leaf Jacobian is J_x=-1-alpha^2/b-(delta/b){2 alpha g'(Z)+delta g'(Z)^2-E g''(Z)}. Thus finiteness of V_x requires the square integrability of g'(Z)^2, which is not implied by the target's declared assumptions. Concretely, g'(z)=exp(3z^2/16) defines a C-infinity mechanism with 0<E[g''(Z)^2]<infinity but E[g'(Z)^4]=infinity; for every delta!=0 the conditional mean of the displayed random term has infinite second moment, so V_x is not a finite R_+-valued functional. The added fourth-moment condition restricts only the tail regime and does not assume the identification conclusion or its positivity.
